political.s: PVH does not directly contribute to political candidates or parties. The company participates in lobbying principally through industry associations. Employee/individual contributions in 2024 totaled approximately $60,297.

charity.s: The PVH Foundation gave approximately $6.7 million annually in recent years, supporting causes including education, women and children's welfare, and COVID-19 relief. The company matches employee donations and offers 48 hours of paid volunteer time.
charity.focus: Education, women and children's welfare, autism awareness, community development

environment.s: PVH has committed to net-zero emissions by 2050 and achieved 50% renewable energy by 2022 (three years ahead of target). The company set ambitious targets: 70% Scope 1&2 emissions reduction by 2030 and 30% Scope 3 reduction. DitchCarbon rated PVH 69/100 for sustainability performance.

labor.s: PVH commits to living wages and worker empowerment in supply chain, covering 86% of global manufacturing base. The company has programs for women's empowerment and safe workplaces, though reports indicate ongoing challenges in achieving equitable compensation and advancement opportunities.

dei.s: PVH achieved 100% on HRC's 2021 Corporate Equality Index and ranked #9 on Fortune's Measure Up DEI list. The company launched nine global I&D commitments and received Innovative recognition from Black in Fashion Council for building inclusive culture and engaging the Black community.

animals.s: As an apparel company, PVH is not primarily engaged in animal testing. Animal testing is not a material issue for fashion/apparel business models.

guns.s: As a fashion and apparel company, PVH does not manufacture, sell, or license firearms.

privacy.s: PVH has comprehensive privacy policies for various stakeholder groups (customers, employees, business partners). The company commits to GDPR compliance and data protection, though data may be shared across PVH branded businesses for analytics and operational purposes.

execPay.s: CEO Stefan Larsson's total compensation is approximately $16.28 million (91.3% in bonuses/stock). Historical data shows CEO pay ratios ranging from 897:1 to 943:1 compared to median employee pay, indicating significant executive compensation disparity.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
